questions.stem_en: A T-intersection warning sign indicates:

options.text_en: An intersection where one road ends at another

questions.explanation_en: A T-intersection sign warns that the road you are on ends ahead. You must turn left or right.
questions.explanation_zh: T型路口标志警告你所在的道路前方结束。你必须左转或右转。

questions.stem_en: A slippery when wet sign warns you that:

options.text_en: There is a water crossing ahead
The road may be slippery when wet

questions.explanation_en: A slippery when wet sign warns the road surface may become slippery in wet conditions. Reduce your speed.
questions.explanation_zh: 湿滑路面标志警告路面在潮湿条件下可能变得湿滑。降低速度。

questions.stem_en: A curve ahead sign warns you to:

options.text_en: Slow down before entering the curve

questions.explanation_en: A curve ahead sign warns of a curve. Reduce speed before entering the curve to maintain control.
questions.explanation_zh: 前方弯道标志警告有弯道。在进入弯道前减速以保持控制。

questions.stem_en: A keep right sign means:

options.text_en: Drive to the right of an obstacle or divider
Slower traffic must use the right lane

questions.explanation_en: A keep right sign indicates you must drive to the right of an obstacle, divider, or median.
questions.explanation_zh: 靠右行驶标志表示你必须在障碍物或分隔带的右侧行驶。

questions.stem_en: A no left turn sign means:

options.text_en: Left turns are prohibited at this location
Left turns allowed only on green arrows
Left turns for emergency vehicles only
Left turns restricted during rush hour

questions.explanation_en: A no left turn sign prohibits left turns at that location.
questions.explanation_zh: 禁止左转标志禁止在该位置左转。

questions.stem_en: A green traffic signal means you may proceed, but you must still:

options.text_en: Yield to vehicles and pedestrians already in the intersection

questions.explanation_en: A green light means GO, but yield to any vehicle, bicyclist, or pedestrian already in the intersection.
questions.explanation_zh: 绿灯表示可以通行，但仍须让行已在路口内的车辆、骑自行车者和行人。

questions.stem_en: A no U-turn sign prohibits you from:

options.text_en: Turning around to go back the way you came

questions.explanation_en: A no U-turn sign means you may not make a U-turn at that location.
questions.explanation_zh: 禁止掉头标志表示在该位置不得掉头。

questions.stem_en: Yellow raised pavement markers on the road indicate:
questions.explanation_en: Yellow raised pavement markers indicate the boundary between traffic traveling in opposite directions.
questions.explanation_zh: 黄色凸起路面标记表示对向行驶车辆之间的分界线。

questions.stem_en: When you reach a roundabout, you must:

options.text_en: Slow down, yield to traffic in the roundabout, and enter when safe

questions.explanation_en: At a roundabout, slow down, yield to all traffic already in the roundabout, and enter when safe.
questions.explanation_zh: 在环形交叉路口，减速，让行已在环形路口内的所有车辆，安全时驶入。

questions.stem_en: When merging onto a highway from an on-ramp, you should:
questions.stem_zhHant: 從匝道匯入高速公路時，您應該：

options.text_en: Stop at the end of the ramp and wait for a gap
options.text_zhHant: 在匝道盡頭停車等待空隙

options.text_en: Be at or near the speed of highway traffic before merging
options.text_zhHant: 在匯入前達到或接近高速公路交通的速度

options.text_en: Merge at any speed and expect others to yield
options.text_zhHant: 以任何速度匯入並期望其他人讓行

options.text_en: Drive slowly until you find a gap

questions.explanation_en: When merging onto a highway, use the on-ramp to accelerate to or near the speed of highway traffic before merging. Do not stop at the end of the ramp unless absolutely necessary.
questions.explanation_zh: 汇入高速公路时，利用匝道加速到或接近高速公路车流速度后再汇入。除非绝对必要，不要在匝道尽头停车。
questions.explanation_zhHant: 匯入高速公路時，利用匝道加速到或接近高速公路車流速度後再匯入。除非絕對必要，不要在匝道盡頭停車。

questions.stem_en: Pedestrians have the right-of-way in:
questions.explanation_en: Pedestrians have the right-of-way in both marked and unmarked crosswalks.
questions.explanation_zh: 行人在标记和未标记的人行横道上都拥有优先通行权。

questions.stem_en: When entering a highway from a merging lane, you should:

options.text_en: Stop at the end of the ramp
Match highway speed and merge safely without stopping
Drive slowly until you find an opening

questions.explanation_en: Be at or near the speed of traffic and merge safely. Do not stop unless absolutely necessary.
questions.explanation_zh: 速度应达到或接近车流速度并安全合并。除非绝对必要，不要停车。

questions.stem_en: When driving in fog, you should use:
questions.stem_zhHant: 在霧中駕駛時，您應該使用：

options.text_zhHant: 遠光燈以獲得最大能見度

questions.explanation_en: When driving in fog, use low-beam headlights. High beams reflect off the fog and create glare, making it even harder to see.
questions.explanation_zh: 在雾中驾驶时，使用近光灯。远光灯会被雾反射产生眩光，使视线更差。
questions.explanation_zhHant: 在霧中駕駛時，使用近光燈。遠光燈會被霧反射產生眩光，使視線更差。

questions.stem_en: End of lane markings on freeways are usually marked with:
questions.explanation_en: Ending lanes are usually marked with large broken lines. Be prepared to exit or merge.
questions.explanation_zh: 车道结束处通常用大的虚线标记。做好驶出或合并的准备。

questions.stem_en: A yield line is a row of solid white triangles showing:

options.text_en: Where vehicles should yield or stop

questions.explanation_en: A yield line shows approaching vehicles where to yield or stop. Triangles point toward approaching vehicles.
questions.explanation_zh: 让行线指示接近的车辆应在哪里让行或停车。

questions.stem_en: A separated bikeway is physically separated from motor vehicles by:

options.text_en: Barriers, flexible posts, or grade separation

questions.explanation_en: A separated bikeway is physically separated from motor vehicles using flexible posts, grade separation, barriers, or on-street parking.
questions.explanation_zh: 隔离自行车道通过柔性柱、坡度分隔、护栏或路边停车与机动车物理分隔。

questions.stem_en: HOV carpool lanes on the road surface are marked with:
questions.explanation_en: HOV lanes are marked with a diamond symbol and the words Carpool Lane. Use designated entrances and exits.
questions.explanation_zh: HOV车道在路面上标有菱形符号和“拼车道”字样。使用指定的入口和出口。

questions.stem_en: The maximum speed limit on a two-lane undivided highway is:
questions.explanation_en: Unless otherwise posted, the speed limit on a two-lane undivided highway is 55 mph.
questions.explanation_zh: 除非另有标示，双车道无隔离带公路的限速为55英里/小时。

questions.stem_en: When passing a bicyclist on a two-lane road and you cannot give 3 feet of space, you should:
questions.stem_zhHant: 在雙車道道路上超越騎自行車的人時，如果您無法提供3英尺的空間，您應該：

options.text_en: Pass quickly to minimize time near the bicyclist
options.text_zhHant: 快速通過以減少靠近騎車者的時間

options.text_en: Honk your horn and pass carefully
Slow down and wait until you can safely give 3 feet
options.text_zhHant: 減速並等到可以安全提供3英尺的空間

options.text_en: Move to the shoulder to pass

questions.explanation_en: If you cannot give a bicyclist at least 3 feet of space when passing, you must slow down to a reasonable speed and wait until you can safely provide at least 3 feet of clearance.
questions.explanation_zh: 如果在超越骑自行车的人时无法提供至少3英尺的空间，你必须减速到合理速度并等到可以安全提供至少3英尺的间距。
questions.explanation_zhHant: 如果在超越騎自行車的人時無法提供至少3英尺的空間，您必須減速到合理速度並等到可以安全提供至少3英尺的間距。

questions.stem_en: Which of the following qualifies you to use an HOV carpool lane?

options.text_en: You have the required number of people or qualifying vehicle
Driving a truck with three or more axles
You need to pass slower traffic

questions.explanation_en: Use an HOV lane if you have the minimum number of occupants posted, drive a qualifying low/zero emission vehicle, or ride a motorcycle.
questions.explanation_zh: 如果你有标志上公布的最少乘员数、驾驶符合条件的低/零排放车辆或骑摩托车，可使用HOV车道。

questions.stem_en: When driving faster or slower than the speed limit, you may be:

options.text_en: Required to take a driving test
Fined only if an accident occurs

questions.explanation_en: You may be cited for driving faster or slower than the speed limit.
questions.explanation_zh: 驾驶速度快于或慢于限速都可能被开罚单。

questions.stem_en: Blind pedestrians using guide dogs or white canes have the right-of-way:

options.text_en: Only when traffic lights are green

questions.explanation_en: Blind pedestrians using guide dogs or white canes have the right-of-way at all times.
questions.explanation_zh: 使用导盲犬或白手杖的盲人行人在任何时候都拥有优先通行权。

questions.stem_en: What rights do bicyclists have on public roads?

options.text_en: The same rights and responsibilities as other drivers

questions.explanation_en: Bicyclists have the same rights and responsibilities as other drivers on public roads.
questions.explanation_zh: 骑自行车的人与其他驾驶员拥有相同的权利和责任。

questions.stem_en: NEVs are restricted from roads with speed limits exceeding:
questions.explanation_en: NEVs reach 25 mph max and are restricted from roads with limits greater than 35 mph.
questions.explanation_zh: NEV最高速度25英里/小时，不得在限速超过35英里/小时的道路上行驶。

questions.stem_en: Driving to the scene of a fire or disaster is illegal because:

options.text_en: You may obstruct emergency personnel and can be arrested
It is only illegal during rush hour
You might get a parking ticket

questions.explanation_en: You can be arrested for driving to an emergency scene as you obstruct rescue personnel.
questions.explanation_zh: 开车到紧急现场可能被逞捕，因为你会妨碍救援人员。

questions.stem_en: You must turn on headlights beginning:

options.text_en: 30 minutes after sunset until 30 minutes before sunrise

questions.explanation_en: Use headlights beginning 30 minutes after sunset until 30 minutes before sunrise.
questions.explanation_zh: 在日落后30分钟开始使用车灯，直到日出前30分钟。

questions.stem_en: Dim high-beam headlights to low beams within how many feet of an oncoming vehicle?
questions.explanation_en: Dim high beams within 500 feet of an oncoming vehicle or 300 feet of a vehicle you are following.
questions.explanation_zh: 在距对面来车500英尺或距你跟随的车辆300英尺范围内切换为近光灯。

questions.stem_en: A flashing DON'T WALK signal means:
questions.stem_zhHant: 閃爍的「不要行走」信號表示：

options.text_en: You may start crossing the street
You should run to cross before it changes
options.text_zhHant: 您應該在信號改變前跑過去

options.text_en: If already crossing, finish crossing; do not start a new crossing
options.text_zhHant: 如果已經在過馬路，繼續走完；不要開始新的穿越

options.text_en: All pedestrians must immediately return to the curb
options.text_zhHant: 所有行人必須立即回到路緣

questions.explanation_en: A flashing DON'T WALK or raised hand signal means if you have already started crossing, finish crossing safely. Do not start a new crossing.
questions.explanation_zh: 闪烁的不要行走或举手信号表示如果你已经开始过马路，安全地走完。不要开始新的穿越。
questions.explanation_zhHant: 閃爍的不要行走或舉手信號表示如果您已經開始過馬路，安全地走完。不要開始新的穿越。

questions.stem_en: What is the legal BAC limit when driving a vehicle that requires a commercial license?
questions.explanation_en: The BAC limit is 0.04% for commercial vehicle drivers and for driving passengers for hire.
questions.explanation_zh: 商用车辆驾驶员和载客收费驾驶员的BAC限制为0.04%。

questions.stem_en: It is illegal to smoke in a vehicle when:

options.text_en: A minor is in the vehicle

questions.explanation_en: You must not smoke when a minor is in the vehicle. You can be fined.
questions.explanation_zh: 当未成年人在车内时不得吸烟。可被罚款。

questions.stem_en: A blue painted curb means parking is reserved for:

options.text_en: Disabled persons with special placards or plates

questions.explanation_en: A blue curb is reserved for disabled persons with a special placard or special license plate.
questions.explanation_zh: 蓝色路缘专为持有特殊停车牌或特殊车牌的残疾人停车保留。

questions.stem_en: You may NOT park on a bridge or in a tunnel unless:
questions.explanation_en: Do not park in a tunnel or on a bridge unless permitted by signs.
questions.explanation_zh: 除非有标志允许，不得在隧道或桥梁上停车。

questions.stem_en: Double parking means:

options.text_en: Parking on the road side of a vehicle already at the curb

questions.explanation_en: Double parking is parking on the road side of a vehicle at the curb. It is illegal.
questions.explanation_zh: 双重停车是停在已停在路缘旁车辆的道路一侧。这是违法的。

questions.stem_en: During a law enforcement traffic break, you should:

options.text_en: Speed up to pass the patrol vehicle
Turn on flashers, match the officer speed, and do not pass
Change lanes to avoid the officer

questions.explanation_en: Turn on emergency flashers, slowly match the officer's speed, and do not pass the patrol vehicle.
questions.explanation_zh: 打开应急闪光灯，慢慢减速到与警察相同速度，不要超越巡逻车。

questions.stem_en: If you encounter flooding on a road, you should:

options.text_en: Drive through quickly to avoid stalling
Find another route if the water is deep
Drive on the shoulder to avoid the water
Follow the vehicle in front of you closely

questions.explanation_en: It may not be possible to determine flood depth by looking. If deep, find another route. If you must drive through, go slowly and test brakes after.
questions.explanation_zh: 可能无法通过观察判断水深。如果水深，找另一条路。如必须通过，慢慢行驶并在之后测试刹车。

questions.stem_en: A minor must have their instruction permit validated by whom before using it?
questions.explanation_en: A minor cannot use their permit until they start training with an instructor who validates it.
questions.explanation_zh: 未成年人在开始跟教练进行实际驾驶培训并由教练验证许可证后才能使用。

questions.stem_en: The provisional license curfew restriction applies for:
questions.explanation_en: The curfew (11 p.m. to 5 a.m.) applies during the first 12 months of having a provisional license.
questions.explanation_zh: 宵禁（11pm-5am）适用于持有临时驾照的前12个月。

questions.stem_en: If a minor has two at-fault collisions or violations within 12 months, DMV may:

options.text_en: Restrict driving for 30 days unless a licensed adult 25+ rides along

questions.explanation_en: Two at-fault incidents means no driving for 30 days unless a licensed adult 25+ accompanies them.
questions.explanation_zh: 两次有过失事件意味着30天内不得驾驶，除非25岁以上的持照成年人陪同。

questions.stem_en: If both parents share joint custody, who must sign the minor's license application?
questions.explanation_en: If parents share joint custody, both must sign the application.
questions.explanation_zh: 如果父母共同拥有监护权，双方都必须签字。

questions.stem_en: A minor must wait how many days to retake a failed behind-the-wheel test?
questions.explanation_en: Minors must wait 14 days (not including the day of failure) to retake a failed drive test.
questions.explanation_zh: 未成年人必须等待14天（不包括失败当天）才能重新参加路考。

questions.stem_en: A parent or guardian may cancel their teen's license by:

options.text_en: Completing a Request for Cancellation form

questions.explanation_en: A parent can cancel by completing a Request for Cancellation or Surrender of a Driver License or ID Card form.
questions.explanation_zh: 父母可以通过填写取消或交回驾照或身份证表格来取消。

questions.stem_en: On the drive test, an accompanying licensed driver for a minor must be at least:
questions.explanation_en: For minors, the accompanying licensed driver must be at least 25 years old.
questions.explanation_zh: 对于未成年人，陪同的持照驾驶员必须至少25岁。

questions.stem_en: A cell phone used while driving by an adult must be mounted on the:

options.text_en: Windshield, dashboard, or center console without blocking the view

questions.explanation_en: Mount your cell phone on the windshield, dashboard, or center console. It cannot block your view. Use single swipe or touch only.
questions.explanation_zh: 将手机安装在挡风玻璃、仪表板或中控台上。不能遮挡视线。只使用单次滑动或触摸功能。

questions.stem_en: Children under 2 years old must be secured in:

options.text_en: A rear-facing child passenger restraint system

questions.explanation_en: Children under 2, under 40 lbs, under 3ft 4in must be in a rear-facing restraint. They may not ride in front of an airbag-equipped vehicle.
questions.explanation_zh: 2岁以下、40磅以下、身高不足3英尺4英寸的儿童必须使用后向式约束系统。

questions.stem_en: During a traffic stop, law enforcement must state the reason for the stop:

options.text_en: Before beginning any questioning about a violation

questions.explanation_en: Officers must state the stop reason before questioning, unless withholding is necessary to protect life or property from imminent threat.
questions.explanation_zh: 执法人员必须在询问前说明停车原因，除非隐瞒原因是为了保护生命或财产免受紧迫威胁。

questions.stem_en: You should never start your vehicle inside a garage with the door closed because:
questions.stem_zhHant: 您不應該在車庫門關閉的情況下啟動車輛，因為：

options.text_en: It can cause carbon monoxide poisoning
options.text_zhHant: 可能導致一氧化碳中毒

options.text_en: It is too loud for neighbors

questions.explanation_en: Never start your vehicle in a closed garage. Carbon monoxide (CO) from the exhaust is colorless and odorless and can cause poisoning or death in an enclosed space.
questions.explanation_zh: 切勿在封闭的车库中启动车辆。排气管排出的一氧化碳（CO）无色无味，在封闭空间中可能导致中毒或死亡。
questions.explanation_zhHant: 切勿在封閉的車庫中啟動車輛。排氣管排出的一氧化碳（CO）無色無味，在封閉空間中可能導致中毒或死亡。

questions.stem_en: Neighborhood Electric Vehicles (NEVs) are restricted from roads where the speed limit exceeds:
questions.stem_zhHant: 社區電動車（NEV）被限制在速度限制超過以下的道路上行駛：
questions.explanation_en: Neighborhood Electric Vehicles (NEVs) have a maximum speed of about 25 mph and are restricted from roads where the speed limit exceeds 35 mph.
questions.explanation_zh: 社区电动车（NEV）最高速度约为25英里/小时，禁止在限速超过35英里/小时的道路上行驶。
questions.explanation_zhHant: 社區電動車（NEV）最高速度約為25英里/小時，禁止在限速超過35英里/小時的道路上行駛。
